@namespace MudBlazor
@inherits MudBaseDatePicker

@Render

@code{

    protected override RenderFragment InputContent=>
        @<MudInputControl Label="@Label" Variant="@Variant" HelperText="@HelperText" Error="@ErrorState.Value"
                          ErrorText="@ErrorTextState.Value" Disabled="@GetDisabledState()" Margin="@Margin" Required="@Required"
                          @onclick="async () => { if (!Editable) await ToggleStateAsync(); }" ForId="@FieldId">
               <InputContent>
                   <MudRangeInput @ref="_rangeInput"
                                  @attributes="UserAttributes"
                                  InputType="@InputType.Text"
                                  Class="@PickerInputClassname"
                                  Style="@Style"
                                  Variant="@Variant"
                                  ReadOnly="@(!Editable)"
                                  Label="@Label"
                                  @bind-Value="@RangeText"
                                  Disabled="@GetDisabledState()"
                                  Adornment="@Adornment"
                                  AdornmentIcon="@AdornmentIcon"
                                  AdornmentColor="@AdornmentColor"
                                  IconSize="@IconSize"
                                  OnAdornmentClick="ToggleStateAsync"
                                  Required="@Required"
                                  RequiredError="@RequiredError"
                                  Error="@ErrorState.Value"
                                  ErrorText="@ErrorTextState.Value"
                                  Margin="@Margin"
                                  AdornmentAriaLabel="@AdornmentAriaLabel"
                                  PlaceholderStart="@PlaceholderStart"
                                  PlaceholderEnd="@PlaceholderEnd"
                                  SeparatorIcon="@SeparatorIcon"
                                  Clearable="@(Clearable && !GetReadOnlyState())"
                                  ClearIcon="@ClearIcon"
                                  Underline="@Underline"
                                  ShrinkLabel="@ShrinkLabel"
                                  InputId="@InputId" />
               </InputContent>
           </MudInputControl>;

}

With `<AsConstants>true</AsConstants>`, the resx entries are exposed as constants on a generated `LanguageResource` class. So each `<data name="X" ...>` entry in the resx file becomes `LanguageResource.X`.

Now I understand the full picture. The bug is:

1. **`MudRangeInput.razor`** - The `aria-label` attributes are missing on the start and end `<input>` elements.
2. **`MudRangeInput.razor.cs`** - Missing `StartInputAriaLabel` and `EndInputAriaLabel` parameters.
3. **`MudDateRangePicker.razor.cs`** - Missing `StartInputAriaLabel` and `EndInputAriaLabel` parameters.
4. **`MudDateRangePicker.razor`** - Missing the `StartInputAriaLabel` and `EndInputAriaLabel` attributes passed to `MudRangeInput`.
5. **`LanguageResource.resx`** - Missing the `MudRangeInput_Start`, `MudRangeInput_End`, `MudDateRangePicker_Start`, `MudDateRangePicker_End` entries.

Now I'll add the `aria-label` attributes. The pattern from the bug description indicates:
- For `MudRangeInput`: when `StartInputAriaLabel` is null, it should default to the localized "Start" text (from `LanguageResource.MudRangeInput_Start`). When it's set, use that value.
- For `MudRangeInput`: when `EndInputAriaLabel` is null, default to localized "End" text. When set, use that value.
- For `MudDateRangePicker`: defaults to "Start date" / "End date" respectively.

In the razor template, we need to provide the aria-label on each input. The expression should be: `StartInputAriaLabel ?? Localizer[LanguageResource.MudRangeInput_Start]` for the start input and similarly for the end.

content.text:
Now let me check the MudDateRangePicker.razor more carefully. The `MudDateRangePicker` renders a `MudRangeInput`, and we need to pass `StartInputAriaLabel` and `EndInputAriaLabel` to it. But the `MudDateRangePicker` has its own default aria labels ("Start date" / "End date").

Looking at the pattern, `MudDateRangePicker` should provide its own localized default aria label values to `MudRangeInput`. The `MudDateRangePicker` has its own `StartInputAriaLabel` and `EndInputAriaLabel` params. When those are null, it should default to the localized `MudDateRangePicker_Start` / `MudDateRangePicker_End` values. But it passes to MudRangeInput, and MudRangeInput itself also has its own defaults.

The key is: `MudDateRangePicker` needs to pass computed values to `MudRangeInput`:
- If `StartInputAriaLabel` is set on `MudDateRangePicker`, use that.
- Otherwise, default to localized "Start date".
- Similar for end.

But `MudRangeInput` itself also uses `StartInputAriaLabel ?? Localizer[...]`. So when `MudDateRangePicker` passes values to `MudRangeInput`, the `MudRangeInput` won't use its own defaults but will use what was passed.

The MudDateRangePicker.razor uses code behind. I need to compute the aria labels that will be passed to MudRangeInput. Let me check the Razor template to understand how to pass:

Looking at the MudDateRangePicker.razor, the MudRangeInput component receives params through the template. I need to add:
```
StartInputAriaLabel="@(StartInputAriaLabel ?? Localizer[LanguageResource.MudDateRangePicker_Start])"
EndInputAriaLabel="@(EndInputAriaLabel ?? Localizer[LanguageResource.MudDateRangePicker_End])"
```

But wait - the `MudDateRangePicker.razor` doesn't inject a Localizer directly. Let me check how the parent class `MudBaseDatePicker` handles this.
